entry.title: Protocol for zero-conf transactions
entry.summary:
The email delves into the exploration and development of innovative blockchain technologies, specifically focusing on zk-statechains and their application in Bitcoin-related projects. The sender is actively engaged in drafting ideas linked to connectors and has been working on a prototype implementation that can be explored further through resources such as [zk-statechains without states](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/zk-statechains-without-states/2166) and [Ciphera - a Bitcoin zk-appchain implementation](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/ciphera-a-bitcoin-zk-appchain-implementation/2285). Additionally, the sender has chosen to focus on a project termed [Blind Vaults version](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/building-a-vault-using-blinded-co-signers/2141), drawing on elements from demo codes previously developed.
The correspondence indicates an interest in potentially using connectors for revoking states in blockchain applications, suggesting this as an alternative to existing schemes like those found in the Lightning Network (LN). However, the sender notes a limitation in research capacity which prevents them from fully exploring this possibility. The sender appreciates how the shared post contributes valuable insights that help bridge the knowledge gaps in their current understanding and ongoing projects, indicating a positive impact on their work. This interaction underscores the collaborative and iterative nature of technological advancements in blockchain implementations.
